title: Ralph Phase N: ternary_matmul compute_kernel_config — real kernel lever
insight: ternary_matmul_nanobind.cpp accepts a compute_kernel_config parameter (defaults to None → init_device_compute_kernel_config). bitlinear.py's Linear class never passes this — so the Q/K/V ternary matmul chain (the documented drift engine from Phase K+L+M) has been running at default fidelity. Passing HiFi4+fp32_dest_acc_en should cost speed but improve PCC.

stories.title: Wire BITNET_TERNARY_MATMUL_HIFI4 env through bitlinear.Linear.__call__
stories.finding: Env flag wired in src/bitnet_tt/layers/bitlinear.py. Default OFF. _hifi4_fp32_kernel_config() helper refactored out of _rmsnorm_compute_kernel_config — shared between RMSNorm and ternary_matmul paths. compute_kernel_config is passed to ttnn.experimental.ternary_matmul when flag is set.
acceptanceCriteria: Env flag read at module import, default OFF — DONE
HiFi4+fp32_acc config passed to ternary_matmul when ON — DONE
Default path unchanged — DONE (verified by 74.35 t/s baseline bench pre-change matching post-change flag=OFF)

stories.title: Measure impact of HiFi4+fp32_acc ternary_matmul on PCC and tps
stories.finding: PURE REGRESSION. HiFi4+fp32_dest_acc on ternary_matmul: decode_tps 71.69 t/s (-2.35 vs 74.04 mean, well outside 0.5 noise band), PCC vs HF fp32 = 0.982045 (IDENTICAL to baseline), PCC vs bitnet.cpp = 0.969858 (IDENTICAL to baseline 0.9699). Zero PCC benefit. The ternary {-1, 0, +1} accumulation is already exact in bf16 because the partial sums never overflow the 8-bit mantissa; HiFi4 upgrade costs cycles but doesn't change the output. Keep env flag default OFF; do NOT make default.
acceptanceCriteria: bench_accuracy with flag=1 — DONE (PCC 0.982045, argmax match)
Decision: PCC delta 0.000 < +0.003 threshold → keep as opt-in env flag (NOT default)

stories.title: Commit env flag + update optimization-ceiling doc
acceptanceCriteria: Session 14 doc updated with N2 finding (ternary_matmul fidelity is a false lever)
